headline: Anthropic: Trump admin lifts export controls on AI models
detail: Easing AI export restrictions is a direct positive for the U.S. AI ecosystem - it expands the addressable market for chips and AI infrastructure globally. Your SMH, SOXL, and CRWD all benefit from a world where AI deployment accelerates without regulatory chokepoints. This is a genuine tailwind for your most concentrated positions.
rec: No trade needed today - you already hold SMH and SOXL which capture this upside. Watch for a clean pullback entry in ALAB (scanner entry $481.96-482.54, stop $479.73) if semi momentum continues; don't chase at current premium.

headline: Small-cap stocks: best first half in 35 years
detail: Small-cap outperformance signals broad risk appetite, not just mega-cap tech dominance - that's a healthy backdrop for your leveraged and growth names. It also means rotation risk: money can flow from large-cap tech into small caps, creating headwinds for your QQQ/SPY if the rotation accelerates. Worth monitoring whether your names keep pace.
rec: No action required now, but if QQQ starts lagging IWM materially, that's a signal to trim some broad-market ETF exposure; hold your stop at $670 on QQQ as the objective line.

headline: China-linked actors target tech as U.S.-China AI competition intensifies
detail: Cyber and geopolitical risk is a persistent overhang for your chip and cybersecurity names - this keeps CRWD's business case very much alive. Escalation here could also trigger export-control reversals that hurt semi names like SMH and SOXL. It's a two-sided story: good for cybersecurity, risk for chip exporters.
rec: CRWD (+6.07%) remains your cleanest beneficiary of this narrative; no reason to trim yet with stop at $590 well below. Hold and let it run unless price action deteriorates.

headline: Trump annual financial disclosure: crypto, Apple stock, celebration coins
detail: Disclosure that Trump holds Apple stock and significant crypto-related income adds a layer of political sensitivity to big-tech and crypto policy going forward - perceived conflicts could influence regulatory decisions affecting your names. Not an immediate trade trigger, but worth noting as a policy wildcard. No direct impact on chips or cybersecurity today.
rec: Nothing actionable here for your book today - this is background political noise. Keep focus on price action in your actual holdings rather than macro speculation.
